_comment: AF option GAIN name -> part-slug, for loadout->variant model resolution (Handover I2, rules v1.1). Match on the option's GAIN name (weapon or rule), NOT the Army-Forge display label (labels carry stats text that changes). Only API-recognizable, NON-default choices are slugs: a replaced weapon (Spear/Halberd/...) and a per-model role (Sergeant/Banner/Musician). The default hand weapon, shields (visual dressing), and Combined-Unit (size only) produce NO slug. Values are Model Forge's baked-variant vocabulary; Model Forge reproduces ModelLibrary.variant_slug (sorted, de-duplicated slugs joined by '+') when naming a variant bake '<baseKey>#<slug>'. Keys matched case-insensitively against a model's distributed loadout item names (= the gain names post-parse). A gain whose slug has no matching '<baseKey>#<slug>' bake falls back to the base model (never a missing lookup), so mapping a gain that is default for one unit but a replacement for another is safe.
_comment_mummified: Mummified Undead go-live (uid t-sIke2snonFSL6Q, gameSystem 4): lines below derived by cross-checking the pilot manifest slug keys against the AF book gains. Single-map conflicts (one gain, different bakes across units) are resolved toward keeping every SLUG reachable: 'great weapon'->greatweapon keeps the greatweapon slug reachable (giant/snakemen), 'heavy lance'->heavylance keeps heavylance reachable (beast riders). The hero/guardian art-specific bakes that then miss (royal champion #lance/#sword, skeleton leader #sword, guardian statues #greatweapon) degrade to the correct base model.
